name: Brock Wright
headline: Blocking-first TE2 with no path to fantasy volume
body:
Wright remains a depth piece in Detroit's offense, and his profile leaves little room for fantasy interest. Across eight 2025 games he ran just 170 routes on a 27.5% route share, drawing 22 targets for a 4% target share and turning them into 14 catches, 108 yards, and two scores. The peripherals confirm the limited role: a 0.064 WOPR, 0.64 yards per route run, and a negative EPA per play (-0.088), down sharply from his efficient -- but equally low-volume -- 2024 (0.192 EPA, 68.8% success). He's a blocker first who lives near the line, with airless usage (one air yard per target).

The path forward looks no clearer. He projects out around 19 catches, 175 yards, and a couple of touchdowns over a full season, the kind of line that never cracks weekly lineups. With Sam LaPorta locked into the receiving snaps and a neck injury clouding his availability, Wright stays a stash-only name. There's nothing here to draft.
